data.title: Kroger misses Q3 sales on lower fuel prices, sale of pharmacy business
data.description:
Shares of Kroger (NYSE:KR) are treading water with a downside bias into Thursday’s open as the grocery chain’s Q3 revenue was impacted by lower fuel prices and the sale of its specialty pharmacy business. The effect was offset by a slight improvement in profits and a marginal increase in expectations for FY24 sales.

The company now expects identical sales ex-fuel to increase by 1.20% to 1.50% from prior guidance of up 0.75% to 1.75%. Kroger (KR) also narrowed its EPS guidance to a range of $4.35 to $4.45 per share from $4.30 to $4.50 per share, versus the consensus estimate of $4.44 per share.

For Q3, Kroger (KR) earned a profit of $0.98 per share, up 3 cents from a year ago and in-line with expectations as revenue dipped 1% to $33.63M, missing estimates by $570M.

FIFO gross margin increased 51 basis points to 22.9% primarily attributable to the sale of Kroger Specialty Pharmacy, brands performance and lower shrink, partially offset by lower pharmacy margins.

Pertaining to the pending merger with Albertsons (ACI), CEO Rodney McMullen gave assurances that Kroger (KR) remains committed to the merger despite regulatory challenges, and paused its share repurchase program to prioritize deleveraging.

“We are committed to closing this merger because bringing Kroger and Albertsons together will provide meaningful and measurable benefits – lower prices, secure jobs and expanded access to fresh, affordable food – for customers, associates, and communities across the country," McMullen said.

Kroger (KR) and Albertsons (ACI) shares are modestly lower in premarket trading.

data.title: Nvidia to open AI center in Vietnam amid Southeast Asia expansion - report
data.description:
Nvidia's (NASDAQ:NVDA) Founder and CEO Jensen Huang signed an agreement to open an AI research and development center in Vietnam, Bloomberg News reported.

At a signing ceremony in Hanoi, Vietnam's Investment Minister Nguyen Chi Dung said that the project, with a Viettel Group data center that uses Nvidia technology, will help in developing advanced AI in the country, the report added.

Huang said that a country's data should be considered its natural and national resource, adding that "Vietnam should process your own data and transform it into Vietnam AI for your industry and society."

Huang's visit comes following his trip to Thailand where he met Prime Minister Paetongtarn Shinawatra on Tuesday and agreed to bolster cooperation with the nation, including help in developing the country's AI infrastructure.

In 2023, Huang told Chinh that Nvidia was looking for a semiconductor center in Vietnam after investing about $250M in the country. In April, the Vietnamese government said Nvidia was reviewing three cities in the nation.

Southeast Asian countries are competing for big tech investments to be data center and AI hubs. Damac Group, backed by billionaire Hussain Sajwani, intends to invest around $3B to build data centers in Malaysia, Indonesia and Thailand over the next three to five years, the report noted.

data.title: Mullen announces commercial EV order from California coffee distributor
data.description:
• Mullen Automotive (NASDAQ:MULN) on Thursday announced an order from Associated Coffee, a San Francisco Bay Area coffee and snack distributor.
  • The initial vehicle order includes 10 Class 3 EV cab chassis trucks and is fulfilled by Papé Group, a West Coast commercial dealer group and an authorized Mullen EV sales and service dealer.
  • MULN +1.33% premarket to $2.28
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: SOL Global acquires an additional $1.5M in solana tokens
data.description:
• SOL Global Investments (OTCPK:SOLCF) announced an additional $1.5M strategic investment in Solana tokens at a price of CDN $331 per token which gives it access to 4533 tokens.
  • The company has engaged Outside The Box Capital, Machai Capital, and Quantum Ventures to perform services for the company.
  • Outside The Box Capital, intends to provide advertising, corporate marketing and investor awareness services.
  • Outside The Box Capital will receive a cash fee of CAD$50,000 for its services. The services are expected to begin on December 5, 2024 and will terminate after one month.
  • Machai Capital intends to provide digital marketing and advertising, as well as search engine optimization and social media marketing management.
  • Machai Capital will receive a cash fee of CDN$150,000 for its services. The campaign is expected to begin on December 9, 2024 and will terminate after three months.
  • Quantum Ventures intends to provide media and marketing creation, and online investor awareness.
  • Quantum Ventures will receive a cash fee of USD$60,000 for its services. The services are expected to begin on December 5, 2024 and terminate after two months.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Insight signs strategic collaboration agreement with AWS
data.description:
• Insight Enterprises (NASDAQ:NSIT) said on Thursday that it has signed a new strategic collaboration agreement with Amazon Web Services to more effectively help clients manage their cloud roadmap, including data and AI strategy.
  • The SCA strengthens Insight’s role as a provider of cloud, data, artificial intelligence, cybersecurity, and intelligent edge solutions across multiple cloud ecosystems.

data.title: RTX's Raytheon awarded $590M production contract for next generation jammer mid-band
data.description:
• Raytheon, an RTX (NYSE:RTX) business, has been awarded a $590 million follow-on production contract from the U.S. Navy for the Next Generation Jammer Mid-Band system.
  • NGJ-MB is a cooperative development and production program with the Royal Australian Air Force (RAAF). The contract includes delivery of shipsets, support equipment, spares and non-recurring engineering support.
  • Work under this contract will take place in McKinney, Texas; Forest, Mississippi; El Segundo, California; and Fort Wayne, Indiana through 2028.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: J-Long Group Limited announces 1-for-10 reverse stock split to regain Nasdaq compliance
data.description:
• J-Long Group Limited (NASDAQ:JL) announced that it will effect a reverse stock split of its ordinary shares at a ratio of 1-for-10, effective as of 11:59 pm on December 9, 2024, in order to regain compliance with the minimum $1.00 bid price per share requirement of Nasdaq.
  • The company's ordinary shares are expected to begin trading on a reverse stock split adjusted basis on Nasdaq as of the open of trading on December 10, 2024.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sabine Royalty Trust declares $0.3111 dividend
data.description:
• Sabine Royalty Trust  (NYSE:SBR) declares $0.3111/share monthly dividend.
  • Forward yield 5.73%
  • Payable Dec. 30; for shareholders of record Dec. 16; ex-div Dec. 16.
  • See SBR Dividend Scorecard, Yield Chart, & Dividend Growth.

data.title: Shift4 cut to Hold at Benchmark over CEO uncertainty
data.description:
Benchmark downgraded Shift4 Payments (NYSE:FOUR) to Hold from Buy after the company's CEO, Jared Isaacman, was nominated to be NASA administrator by President-elect Donald Trump. On Wednesday, BofA also cut its recommendation on the stock to Neutral.

"We view Mr. Isaacman’s pending departure as representing the realization of the key person risk that has been one of FOUR’s primary vulnerabilities since it went public four-and-a-half years ago," analyst Mark Palmer wrote in a note to clients.

Isaacman's acceptance of the nomination gives rise to "significant uncertainty regarding the company’s ability to execute its strategy as effectively as it has up to this point," the analyst added.

Although Isaacman didn't explicitly name Shift4 (NYSE:FOUR) President Taylor Lauber as his successor, he said in a letter to employees that he is confident in Lauber and "the team" to execute the company's growth strategy, Palmer noted.

Benchmark's Hold rating on FOUR aligns with the SA Quant rating of Hold and contrasts with the average Wall Street rating of Strong Buy and average SA Analyst rating of Buy.

Shift4 (FOUR) stock rose 1.2% in Thursday premarket trading after dropping 12% on Wednesday.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Kroger Q3 misses top-line estimates; narrows FY24 outlook
